heading: Use of amounts
label: 300ff–14 Use of amounts
notes: References in TextThe Social Security Act, referred to in subsecs. (f)(2) and (g)(1), is , . Titles XIX and XXI of the Act are classified generally to subchapters XIX (§ 1396 et seq.) and XXI (§ 1397aa et seq.), respectively, of chapter 7 of this title. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see and Tables.act Aug. 14, 1935, ch. 53149 Stat. 620section 1305 of this title

Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “For the purpose of providing health and support services to infants, children, and women with HIV disease, including treatment measures to prevent the perinatal transmission of HIV, the chief elected official of an eligible area, in accordance with the established priorities of the planning council, shall use, from the grants made for the area under for a fiscal year, not less than the percentage constituted by the ratio of the population in such area of infants, children, and women with acquired immune deficiency syndrome to the general population in such area of individuals with such syndrome.”Pub. children.text: subject to paragraph (2), the allocation of funds and services within the eligible area will be made in accordance with the priorities established, pursuant to , by the HIV health services planning council that serves such eligible area;section 300ff–12(b)(4)(C) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: core medical services described in subsection (c);

children.label: B
children.text: support services described in subsection (d); and

children.label: C
children.text: administrative expenses described in subsection (h); and
the use of such funds will comply with the requirements of this section.

children.heading: Direct financial assistance to appropriate entities
children.label: b Direct financial assistance to appropriate entities

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The chief elected official of an eligible area shall use amounts from a grant under to provide direct financial assistance to entities described in paragraph (2) for the purpose of providing core medical services and support services.section 300ff–11 of this title

children.heading: Appropriate entities
children.label: 2 Appropriate entities
children.text: Direct financial assistance may be provided under paragraph (1) to public or nonprofit private entities, or private for-profit entities if such entities are the only available provider of quality HIV care in the area.

children.heading: Required funding for core medical services
children.label: c Required funding for core medical services

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: With respect to a grant under for an eligible area for a grant year, the chief elected official of the area shall, of the portion of the grant remaining after reserving amounts for purposes of paragraphs (1) and (5)(B)(i) of subsection (h), use not less than 75 percent to provide core medical services that are needed in the eligible area for individuals with HIV/AIDS who are identified and eligible under this subchapter (including services regarding the co-occurring conditions of the individuals).section 300ff–11 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: there are no waiting lists for AIDS Drug Assistance Program services under ; andsection 300ff–26 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: core medical services are available to all individuals with HIV/AIDS identified and eligible under this subchapter.

children.heading: Notification of waiver status
children.label: B Notification of waiver status
children.text: When informing the chief elected official of an eligible area that a grant under is being made for the area for a grant year, the Secretary shall inform the official whether a waiver under subparagraph (A) is in effect for such year.section 300ff–11 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: Outpatient and ambulatory health services.

children.label: B
children.text: AIDS Drug Assistance Program treatments in accordance with .section 300ff–26 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: AIDS pharmaceutical assistance.

children.label: D
children.text: Oral health care.

children.label: E
children.text: Early intervention services described in subsection (e).

children.label: F
children.text: Health insurance premium and cost sharing assistance for low-income individuals in accordance with .section 300ff–25 of this title

children.label: G
children.text: Home health care.

children.label: H
children.text: Medical nutrition therapy.

children.label: I
children.text: Hospice services.

children.label: J
children.text: Home and community-based health services as defined under .section 300ff–24(c) of this title

children.label: K
children.text: Mental health services.

children.label: L
children.text: Substance abuse outpatient care.

children.label: M
children.text: Medical case management, including treatment adherence services.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “support services” means services, subject to the approval of the Secretary, that are needed for individuals with HIV/AIDS to achieve their medical outcomes (such as respite care for persons caring for individuals with HIV/AIDS, outreach services, medical transportation, linguistic services, and referrals for health care and support services).

children.heading: Medical outcomes
children.label: 2 Medical outcomes
children.text: In this subsection, the term “medical outcomes” means those outcomes affecting the HIV-related clinical status of an individual with HIV/AIDS.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “early intervention services” means HIV/AIDS early intervention services described in , with follow-up referral provided for the purpose of facilitating the access of individuals receiving the services to HIV-related health services. The entities through which such services may be provided under the grant include public health departments, emergency rooms, substance abuse and mental health treatment programs, detoxification centers, detention facilities, clinics regarding sexually transmitted diseases, homeless shelters, HIV/AIDS counseling and testing sites, health care points of entry specified by eligible areas, federally qualified health centers, and entities described in that constitute a point of access to services by maintaining referral relationships.section 300ff–51(e) of this titlesection 300ff–52(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: Federal, State, or local funds are otherwise inadequate for the early intervention services the entity proposes to provide; and

children.label: B
children.text: the entity will expend funds pursuant to such paragraph to supplement and not supplant other funds available to the entity for the provision of early intervention services for the fiscal year involved.

children.heading: Priority for women, infants, children, and youth
children.label: f Priority for women, infants, children, and youth

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For the purpose of providing health and support services to infants, children, youth, and women with HIV/AIDS, including treatment measures to prevent the perinatal transmission of HIV, the chief elected official of an eligible area, in accordance with the established priorities of the planning council, shall for each of such populations in the eligible area use, from the grants made for the area under for a fiscal year, not less than the percentage constituted by the ratio of the population involved (infants, children, youth, or women in such area) with HIV/AIDS to the general population in such area of individuals with HIV/AIDS.section 300ff–11(a) of this title

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: 2 Waiver
children.text: With respect to the population involved, the Secretary may provide to the chief elected official of an eligible area a waiver of the requirement of paragraph (1) if such official demonstrates to the satisfaction of the Secretary that the population is receiving HIV-related health services through the State medicaid program under title XIX of the Social Security Act [ et seq.], the State children’s health insurance program under title XXI of such Act [ et seq.], or other Federal or State programs.42 U.S.C. 139642 U.S.C. 1397aa

children.heading: Requirement of status as medicaid provider
children.label: g Requirement of status as medicaid provider
A
children.text: the political subdivision involved will provide the service directly, and the political subdivision has entered into a participation agreement under the State plan and is qualified to receive payments under such plan; or

children.label: B
children.text: the political subdivision will enter into an agreement with a public or nonprofit private entity under which the entity will provide the service, and the entity has entered into such a participation agreement and is qualified to receive such payments.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of an entity making an agreement pursuant to paragraph (1)(B) regarding the provision of services, the requirement established in such paragraph shall be waived by the HIV health services planning council for the eligible area if the entity does not, in providing health care services, impose a charge or accept reimbursement available from any third-party payor, including reimbursement under any insurance policy or under any Federal or State health benefits program.

children.heading: Determination
children.label: B Determination
children.text: A determination by the HIV health services planning council of whether an entity referred to in subparagraph (A) meets the criteria for a waiver under such subparagraph shall be made without regard to whether the entity accepts voluntary donations for the purpose of providing services to the public.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 1 Limitation
children.text: The chief elected official of an eligible area shall not use in excess of 10 percent of amounts received under a grant under this subpart for administrative expenses.

children.heading: Allocations by chief elected official
children.label: 2 Allocations by chief elected official
children.text: In the case of entities and subcontractors to which the chief elected official of an eligible area allocates amounts received by the official under a grant under this subpart, the official shall ensure that, of the aggregate amount so allocated, the total of the expenditures by such entities for administrative expenses does not exceed 10 percent (without regard to whether particular entities expend more than 10 percent for such expenses).

children.label: A
children.text: routine grant administration and monitoring activities, including the development of applications for part A funds, the receipt and disbursal of program funds, the development and establishment of reimbursement and accounting systems, the development of a clinical quality management program as described in paragraph (5), the preparation of routine programmatic and financial reports, and compliance with grant conditions and audit requirements; and

children.label: B
children.text: all activities associated with the grantee’s contract award procedures, including the activities carried out by the HIV health services planning council as established under , the development of requests for proposals, contract proposal review activities, negotiation and awarding of contracts, monitoring of contracts through telephone consultation, written documentation or onsite visits, reporting on contracts, and funding reallocation activities.section 300ff–12(b) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: usual and recognized overhead activities, including established indirect rates for agencies;

children.label: B
children.text: management oversight of specific programs funded under this subchapter; and

children.label: C
children.text: other types of program support such as quality assurance, quality control, and related activities.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: A Requirement
children.text: The chief elected official of an eligible area that receives a grant under this subpart shall provide for the establishment of a clinical quality management program to assess the extent to which HIV health services provided to patients under the grant are consistent with the most recent Public Health Service guidelines for the treatment of HIV/AIDS and related opportunistic infection, and as applicable, to develop strategies for ensuring that such services are consistent with the guidelines for improvement in the access to and quality of HIV health services.

children.label: I
children.text: 5 percent of amounts received under the grant; or

children.heading: Relation to limitation on administrative expenses
children.label: ii Relation to limitation on administrative expenses
children.text: The costs of a clinical quality management program under subparagraph (A) may not be considered administrative expenses for purposes of the limitation established in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Construction
children.label: i Construction
children.text: A chief elected official may not use amounts received under a grant awarded under this subpart to purchase or improve land, or to purchase, construct, or permanently improve (other than minor remodeling) any building or other facility, or to make cash payments to intended recipients of services.
